clinical_validity.rationale: The first vertical slice preserves the clinically realistic ED chest pain station.

psychometric_defensibility.rationale: Trace and review workflow remain first-class in the implementation plan.

technical_feasibility.rationale: Package ownership and local-first sequencing make the build more feasible.

architecture_coherence.rationale: The package map cleanly follows domain, runtime, gateway, app, and test boundaries.

security_privacy.rationale: Mock providers and no-cloud defaults keep data flow bounded in the first build.

ux_workflow_fit.rationale: Admin and learner shell work are included in the vertical slice.

cost_performance_efficiency.rationale: The implementation plan avoids paid services and live generation.

open_source_sustainability.rationale: Dependencies remain mostly standard TypeScript packages with optional local AI gates.

market_gtm_strength.rationale: A working ED station demo strengthens design-partner conversations.

evidence_discipline.rationale: The plan distinguishes code tasks from unresolved runtime evidence.

implementation_readiness.rationale: The implementation plan has a concrete package map and first milestone.

adversarial_robustness.rationale: The adversarial team constrained package growth to first-slice value.

legal_regulatory_resilience.rationale: No-cloud, mock-first, human-reviewed posture stays intact.

specialty_clinical_generalizability.rationale: The first slice is narrow but preserves expansion paths.

critical_risks.summary: The package map could still feel large unless workers implement it strictly by vertical-slice tests.

evidence_debt.summary: Validate dependency install and package workspace behavior on a clean checkout.

decision_debt.summary: Choose whether Bun is installed before or after the first Node-compatible Hono API skeleton.

summary: Iteration 0005 improves code implementation readiness through package ownership, vertical-slice sequencing, and local-first dependency gates.
